frameworks.name: nonlinear dynamical systems
frameworks.one_line: neural circuit modeled as nonlinear dynamical system to steer activity

frameworks.name: control theory
frameworks.one_line: control-theoretic design of multi-site stimulation patterns

frameworks.name: recurrent network models
frameworks.one_line: RNN circuit model used to demonstrate perturbation-design feasibility

core_question: Can a spatiotemporal pattern of multi-site stimulation be designed to steer neural activity and behavior toward a desired target?
key_assumptions: effective perturbation hubs are identifiable from spontaneous activity
perturbation sites must satisfy both efficacy and selectivity criteria
circuit dynamics are tractable as a nonlinear dynamical system amenable to control
manipulations: choice of perturbation site (hub vs non-hub)
spatiotemporal pattern of multi-site microstimulation in RNN models
key_findings: hubs can be identified from short observations of spontaneous neural activity
combining dynamical systems, control theory, and data-driven methods yields efficient multi-site stimulation designs
approach is feasible in recurrent network model demonstrations
interpretations: model-based design can replace laborious trial-and-error perturbation selection
two-step pipeline (hub identification then control-theoretic pattern design) enables targeted causal manipulation at high spatiotemporal precision
limitations_noted: feasibility shown only in recurrent network model simulations, not in vivo
